focus: Confirm cleanup-asymmetry diff is exhaustive; audit _bg_index fire-and-forget pattern in cli.py and server.py
noveltyJustification: Two new MCP-child-side findings (F-017, F-018) plus a tightened refinement of F-014 (F-016); cluster reframing of F-005/F-010 to span daemon AND MCP child processes is new.
summary: Cleanup-asymmetry diff confirmed exhaustive at 7 caches; only _embedder_by_config_hash is a true deterministic leak under refresh. Fire-and-forget _bg_index repeated at cli.py:1204 and server.py:557 with bare except + ThreadPoolExecutor hazard.

title: _refresh_project_if_config_changed true leak is the previous-hash embedder; other missing pops are overwritten on next get_project
cli.py:1204 _bg_index task is fire-and-forget; bare except + ThreadPoolExecutor worker survives task cancel
server.py:557 _bg_index is an exact mirror of F-017 in the alternate _serve MCP entrypoint
ProjectRegistry registers exactly 7 caches; cleanup-asymmetry diff is exhaustive
F-014 narrowed: true deterministic leak in _refresh_project_if_config_changed is the previous-hash embedder; _index_locks, _load_time_done, _current_index_meta are self-healing via overwrite
Fire-and-forget cluster expands beyond daemon: daemon.py:378 + cli.py:1204 + server.py:557 are three instances of the same defect

note: Correct-discipline reference pattern lives at daemon.py:1244-1255 (handler tasks): task_set.add(task) + task.add_done_callback(task_set.discard), then asyncio.wait_for(asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True), timeout=10.0) at lines 1286-1295. Remediation for daemon.py:378 / cli.py:1204 / server.py:557 should follow this pattern.

_bg_index is duplicated byte-for-byte (modulo type hint) between cli.py:1210-1218 and server.py:563-569. Remediation packet should consolidate into a single shared helper before applying the task-lifecycle fix, otherwise the two copies will drift.

Both _bg_index sites use loop.run_in_executor(None, client.index, project_root). The default ThreadPoolExecutor worker is NOT cancelled when the wrapping asyncio Task is cancelled. asyncio.run drains it via loop.shutdown_default_executor on exit, which means MCP child SIGTERM can be stalled by an in-flight client.index RPC. Iteration 004 should verify the blocking semantics of DaemonClient.index.

hypothesis: ProjectRegistry has an eighth registry-scope cache hiding outside iter-002's seven-cache enumeration
reason: daemon.py:342-356 is the complete attribute set declared on the class. Read end-to-end. No additional dict / set / list bound in __init__ outside this block.

hypothesis: server.py contains additional fire-and-forget create_task sites beyond line 557
reason: Grep for asyncio.(create_task|gather|wait_for|shield|add_done_callback) in server.py returned create_task at 557 only; wait_for at 266 and 345 are correctly-bound timeout dispatchers, not background-task sites.
